methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $300M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Cemvita Factory is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Moji Karimi, currently valued at $120M post-money on $30M total raised. The peer universe comprises five private climate-tech companies at Series B stage—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—alongside one public comparable (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, implies $228M from the $120M base valuation. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $400M, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercial stage within the climate-tech cohort.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $194M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $314M. This implies 2.6x upside to the low end and 1.5x upside to the median, relative to current valuation. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to peers, path to revenue, and capital intensity of commercialization. The peer set's wide dispersion ($300M to $5.0B) underscores sensitivity to assumptions about commercial traction and addressable market, limiting precision within this range.
